items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: Which chords form a plagal cadence?
items.why: Students confuse plagal (IV-I) with perfect (V-I) because both end on I. The 'amen' at the end of hymns is the classic plagal cadence - subdominant to tonic, with a softer ecclesiastical feel.

items.q: Which recitative type uses only continuo accompaniment?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Recitativo secco. “Recitativo accompagnato” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Handel's heroic opera roles such as Rinaldo were typically written for which voice type?
items.why: It's Castrato. “Tenor” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Handel compose 'Rinaldo', his first London opera?
items.why: The real figure is 1711. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
